data.title: Transcript: Sensient Technologies Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
data.description:
Sensient Technologies (NYSE:SXT) released first-quarter financial results and hosted an earnings call on Friday. Read the complete transcript below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

View the webcast at https://event.choruscall.com/mediaframe/webcast.html?webcastid=DBLH9ujm

Summary

Sensient Technologies reported strong first quarter results with 7% local currency revenue growth, 10% adjusted EBITDA growth, and 14% adjusted EPS growth.

The Color group led performance with 12.3% revenue growth, driven by strong demand for natural color conversions.

The company is focused on the wholesale conversion of synthetic to natural colors in the U.S., aiming for a $1 billion sales goal.

Sensient Technologies is increasing its full-year guidance, expecting high single to double-digit local currency revenue growth.

Management highlighted ongoing investments in production capacity, supply chain resilience, and research to support long-term growth.

Full Transcript

Tobin Tornell (Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Paul Manning (Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer)

Tobin Tornell (Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

OPERATOR

Thank you. We will now begin the question and answer session. To ask a question, you may press Star then one on your touchtone phone. To withdraw your question, please press Star then two. And at this time we'll pause momentarily to assemble our roster. And the first question will come from Ghasham Pajabi with beard. Please go ahead.

Ghasham Pajabi (Equity Analyst)

Hey guys. Good morning. Hey Ghasham. Morning. Morning, Paul. It sounded like the first quarter came in better than you thought. Just maybe give us a bit more color, pun intended I guess. On what drove that was There just faster conversions of customers. Was there a bigger contribution from load in benefit as it relates to inventory build, et cetera? Just give us a bit more perspective on that.

Paul Manning (Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer)

Ghasham Pajabi (Equity Analyst)

And then as it relates to the cadence of growth in that segment as the year unfolds, I mean obviously very strong start to 1Q. You know, you don't have full control in terms of business wins, et cetera, but presumably you have some view on backlogs, et cetera. How should we think about the cadence of that as it relates to 2Q through 4Q specific to your overall guidance for that segment?

Paul Manning (Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer)

Ghasham Pajabi (Equity Analyst)

Okay. And then just one final one on the TiO2 opportunity set. Can you sort of frame that for us as it relates to how big that is, you know, in terms of the addressable market, et cetera? And is that part of the billion dollar sales threshold that you're focused on in terms of natural colors, or is that separate from that?

Paul Manning (Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer)

Ghasham Pajabi (Equity Analyst)

Thanks so much, and congrats on the progress.

Paul Manning (Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer)

Yep. Thank you.

The next question will come from Josh Specter with ubs. Please go ahead.

Got it. Thank you.

The next question will come from Larry Solow with CGS Securities. Please go ahead.

Good morning. Paul Tobin, Congrats. Hey, Larry. Yeah, thank you.

First one is a quick, simple one. I was wondering if you could give us an update on the revenue related to the conversion of synthetic colors. I think last quarter you were at about $5 million. If you give an update as of this quarter.

The 1 billion.

Okay. Thank you for your time today. That concludes our call. If you have any follow up questions, please feel free to reach out to the company. Have a great weekend.

data.title: Why Scinai Immunotherapeutics Stock Is Skyrocketing 90% Higher Today
data.description:
• Scinai Immunotherapeutics stock is showing downward pressure. What’s the outlook for SCNI shares?

What Is Scinai’s $2.61 Million Financing Catalyst?

Scinai announced about $2.61 million in gross proceeds from a private placement financing plus a warrant inducement, with closing expected on or about April 27, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

The warrant inducement covers immediate exercise of up to 229,310 ADSs at 48 cents per ADS, paired with new unregistered warrants for up to 458,621 ADSs at 55 cents per ADS that expire five years from issuance.

The company said it plans to use net proceeds (alongside existing cash) to expand its CDMO platform, advance customer programs, and keep selectively investing in its immunotherapy pipeline.

SCNI Stock: Critical Levels To Watch

Scinai is trying to stabilize off its April swing low while still sitting far below its longer-term trend, which keeps the bigger-picture chart fragile. The stock is trading 43.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 3.6% above its 100-day SMA, which leans toward a short-term rebound that's only starting to test intermediate trend pressure.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 25.74, which is in oversold territory and lines up with the oversold signal that appeared in April. RSI at 25.74 reflects heavy recent selling pressure, even if bounces can show up quickly from these levels.

The 12-month return is -65.70%, which is consistent with a longer-term downtrend that hasn't been repaired yet. That longer-term damage also shows up in the moving-average structure, with the 50-day SMA still below the 200-day SMA (a bearish long-term alignment) and price still below the 200-day SMA.

  • Key Resistance: $1.00 — a round-number area that often caps early rebound attempts
  • Key Support: 50 cents — near the lower end of the recent trading zone

SCNI Stock Price Activity On Friday

SCNI Stock Price Activity: Scinai Immunotherapeutics shares are trading higher by 92.11% to 87 cents at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Adobe Stock Images

data.title: S&P 500 Is Overbought—But JPMorgan Says The Rally Isn't Done Yet
data.description:
The S&P 500 index has pushed to fresh highs on April 23, but the pace of the move is starting to raise eyebrows. Short-term momentum is stretched, and parts of the market—especially growth and tech—are looking crowded.

Still, JPMorgan analyst Jason Hunter says the rally isn't flashing the kind of signals that typically mark a top.

S&P 500: Overbought, Not Exhausted

Hunter notes that while the recent surge has driven overbought readings to levels last seen late last year, price action isn't showing signs of fatigue. That distinction matters. Markets can stay overbought for longer than expected, especially when trend momentum remains intact.

A deeper break below the 6,700–6,600 zone would be needed to meaningfully disrupt the current uptrend—but for now, that risk remains distant.

From Breakout To Slow Grind

What may be changing is the pace of the rally. JPMorgan expects the market to begin decelerating in the weeks ahead as the index approaches a key resistance band in the 7,100–7,300 range, where longer-term trend lines could start to cap gains.

That doesn't necessarily mean a pullback—it could just mean a shift from a sharp, momentum-driven surge to a slower, more measured climb.

A similar pattern played out late last year, when stocks continued higher but at a more gradual pace.

A More Selective Market Ahead

That kind of narrow leadership isn't always ideal—but it hasn't derailed the broader index.

Instead, it suggests a market that may become more selective, with fewer stocks doing the heavy lifting.

For investors tracking the index through ETFs like iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSE:IVV), the message is straightforward: the uptrend is intact, but it's becoming more dependent on holding key support levels.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Cohere, Aleph Alpha Merge, Schwarz Group Companies Invest $600 Million
data.description:
Canada-based Cohere is merging with Germany-based Aleph Alpha after nearly a year of talks regarding the acquisition.

The transatlantic alliance, which will be anchored in both Germany and Canada, will combine Cohere's global AI scale with Aleph Alpha's research and institutional relationships, a press release stated. 

• Why is SPY stock breaking out?

The combined offering will focus on customized deployments for areas such as public sector work, finance, defense, energy, manufacturing, telecom and health care.

"Combining the strengths of Cohere and Aleph Alpha accelerates our global expansion and advances our mission to deliver sovereign AI to nations around the world," said Aidan Gomez, co-founder and CEO of Cohere.

A McKinsey report noted that "the market for AI services is projected to surpass $1 trillion annually, with sovereign AI needs representing nearly $600 billion of that total."

"The partnership uniquely bridges the gap between these segments with its sovereign-first approach, capturing the critical intersection where sovereignty requirements meet broad enterprise AI adoption," the press release continued.

As part of the plan, the companies said they will work with Schwarz Group companies to deliver the sovereign setup through STACKIT, a Schwarz Digitscloud platform. 

In addition, the companies of Schwarz Group plan to back Cohere's upcoming Series E funding as a lead investor with a $600 million structured financing commitment. The round is understood to have attracted strong interest from leading investors, the company stated.

In a joint statement, Rolf Schumann and Christian Müller, co-CEOs of Schwarz Digits, said: "With this investment, the companies of Schwarz Group position themselves as lead investors for digital sovereignty and infrastructure. Building this infrastructure is a strategic necessity to help shape the AI revolution based on values such as trust, fairness, and responsibility.”

“The establishment of STACKIT, Schwarz Digits' sovereign cloud infrastructure, as the technical backbone of this transatlantic AI initiative, empowers organizations to strengthen their digital independence and maintain control over their data. This is true leadership in digital sovereignty," the co-CEOs added.

It was previously reported the merger will have the support of Berlin, as the German government would be interested in becoming a "key customer" of the combined company.

Aleph Alpha told Reuters that it routinely holds discussions about potential strategic partnerships, as is common across the AI sector, and emphasized that it maintains an independent strategy.

Founded in 2019, Cohere specializes in Large Language Models (LLMs) and generative AI with a specific focus on serving the enterprise market. The company builds AI technology tailored for businesses, focusing on security, data privacy and customizability.

Aleph Alpha, also founded in 2019, specializes in developing sovereign, explainable and reliable LLMs and multimodal AI tailored for complex, mission-critical applications in regulated industries like public administration, defense, and manufacturing.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: The Hartford Insurance Gr Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
The Hartford Insurance Gr (NYSE:HIG) reported first-quarter financial results on Friday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/517599819

Summary

The Hartford Insurance Gr reported strong Q1 2026 results with core earnings of $866 million and a core earnings ROE of 20.3%.

Business Insurance saw a 6% written premium growth with an underlying combined ratio of 89.2, highlighting underwriting discipline and strategic pricing.

The company continues to focus on expanding its small business ecosystem and leveraging technology to enhance underwriting processes.

In Personal Insurance, the underlying combined ratio improved, though growth was impacted by a competitive market; new product rollouts in the agency channel are progressing well.

Employee Benefits reported a core earnings margin of 6.9%, driven by strong life and disability performance and increased sales activity.

Investment income remains robust, supported by a diversified portfolio with a focus on maintaining strong credit quality.

Management emphasized maintaining discipline in pricing and underwriting amidst competitive pressures and technological advancements.

The company plans to maintain its share repurchase program, with $1.1 billion remaining authorized through December 2026.

Full Transcript

Kate Jordan

Mo

Our next question comes from Mike Zaremski from BMO Capital Markets. Please go ahead. Your line is open.

So I'm just kind of pivoting just for specifically to global specialty. Since you called pricing being up a bit. Just curious what caused that, if it's worth talking about. Thanks.

Yeah, I would say moen, you can add your color. I would say wholesale, particularly on the primary liability side and a little excess casualty where the team is really being disciplined and focused on getting rate. As I said with anything with liability in the product line, we are super focused on getting the needed rate. But mo any color, I think that's the key point. Mike, if you think financial lines is it is where it has been. We didn't feel dramatic change Marines the same. We have a lot of lines within that global specialty book, but I think the standout in the quarter was the rate coming back up in wholesale casualty lines.

Yeah, I think this is where the balance of power is actually really equal. Because of all the capabilities we bring. There's very few people who can bring what we bring in the small business space.
